description.value:
Used as a replacement for skills rolled with base COOL, EMP, or WILL

Used for rolling in checks which involve force of will, charisma, general intelligence, or intution. Note that if the check is specialized and the mook is not, the DV should be raised 1-3 tiers.

Used as a replacement for fighting or ranged weapon skills, excluding evasion. Note that this uses REF as a base for reasons involving initiative - set REF of the mook first, then consider that number when considering the Martial skill.
Used as a replacement for skills which rely on base Body or Dex, which are not covered by the Martial skill, and for Evasion.
Used for skills which rely on base INT or TECH.
Medium version of an Armorjack for your head. -2 to REF, DEX, and MOVE.
Medium version of an Armorjack. -2 to REF, DEX, and MOVE.
Built in agent. Audio only but can be linked with other cybergear.
A shield with a bit more mass, used for riot control. Requires BODY 7 to wield effectively.
Deals no Damage. Hit targets with Meat eyes roll DV13 Resist Torture/Drugs Check. On fail suffer Damaged Eye Critical Injury (See pg. 188) for 1 minute. Does not deal critical bonus damage.

Anytime the stun baton deals damage, the attack target must make an Endurance check: DV = 10+dmg (max DV: 16). On failure, the target's MOVE is reduced by 4 and all actions they attempt suffer a -4 penalty until the end of their next turn. An actor can only suffer from one instance of this effect during each of their turns.

information.notes:
Mook Tier - Tier 4/Elite Threat - Mid-level, named threats, who run the bulk of activity for most orgs within NC. If a PC didn't choose to become an edgerunner, they probably would've eventually ended up in a position like this instead.

Ability - Stop Resisting: Immune to the effects of teargas grenades. When attacking a target which is unconscious, prone, grappled, restrained, or being affected by a Teargas Grenade or Stun Stick's ability, ranged attack damage ignores half armor, and melee attack damage ignores all armor.

Extra Gear: A typical SWAT squad is made up of eight members - four strike, four suppressor - while perimeter control agents are typically suppressors. They arrive in an NCPD Armored Transport. Choose one of the two packages for each SWAT officer.

  • Strike Package: Equipped with an assault rifle with 75 basic rifle rounds, or a shotgun with 12 basic shells & 8 rubber slugs.

  • Suppressor Package: Equipped with a bulletproof glass shield (20 hp) & a teargas grenade.

Gang-Wide Ability - Threat Rating: When the NCPD is first alerted to a disturbance in a district, a threat rating is immediately assigned. When the reinforcements at that tier of threat are defeated, forced to retreat, or assess the situation, the threat rating increases by 1 or more, and the reinforcements associated with that rating are immediately dispatched, arriving in 1d3 minutes.

Threat rating is not assigned in combat zones, the reclaimed perimeter or the NorCal Military Base, and cannot exceed 2 in the overpacked suburbs.

  • Tier 1: No reinforcements are sent. Created when an NCPD officer is engaged or feels threatened.

  • Tier 2 - Armed Response: two NCPD Patrol Cars are dispatched, each containing two Patrol Officers.

  • Tier 3 - Perimeter Control: three NCPD Patrol Cars are dispatched, each containing three Patrol Officers and a SWAT Officer. These officers will create a perimeter and assess the situation while attempting to prevent anyone from leaving the scene, but will not directly engage agitators unless they attempt to breach the perimeter, instead calling in a Suppression team to take down dangerous players while notifying MaxTac to remain on standby.

  • Tier 4 - Suppression Dispatch: an NCPD Armored Transport is dispatched, containing an eight-person team of SWAT Officers.

  • Tier 5 - MaxTac Response: An A/V-4 is dispatched, containing a four person team of MaxTac Officers. They do not negotiate, do not discriminate, do not take bribes, and do not take prisoners if the perps don’t surrender peacefully. They are the law.

You want proof of NCPD corruption, look no further! SWAT Officers, pah - riot control for when the idiot workers get up in arms over pay cuts, and illegal enforcement for corpo-sanctioned raids on gangers or fixers. They barely get any funding, so it’s up to their “appropriations” and “charitable donations” to keep the lights on. Protect and serve, my ass!
